jobs.title: Datacenter NetDeploy Lead - Stargate
jobs.body: About the Team OpenAI, in close collaboration with our capital partners, is building the world’s most advanced AI infrastructure ecosystem. This team defines and executes our core infrastructure strategy. From site selection through full buildout, we sit at the intersection of commercial, technical, strategy, and operations, partnering closely with internal teams and external stakeholders. About the Role We are seeking a Datacenter NetDeploy Lead to own the end-to-end execution of physical network deployments in data center environments. This role bridges Network Engineering, Capacity Delivery/Construction, Data Center Engineering, and Operations to ensure network infrastructure is installed, validated, and formally handed over in a production-ready state. The NetDeploy Lead is accountable for vendor execution, schedule delivery, quality standards, and in-field issue resolution, while maintaining strict alignment to documented standards, ticketing, cleanliness, and escalation protocols. Key Responsibilities - Lead full lifecycle physical network deployments for new builds, expansions, and refreshes. - Translate network topologies, cabling designs, and BOMs into executable field plans and sequencing. - Oversee and/or coordinate rack-and-stack of network equipment (switches, OOB, ancillary network systems). - Oversee and/or coordinate fiber trunk pulls, routing, and termination to patch panels / equipment. - Oversee and/or coordinate patch panel installation, patching, labeling, and cable management. - Drive port-to-port verification, continuity testing, and validation sign-off. - Ensure work follows documented standards and runbooks (tables, tickets, checklists, cleanliness, escalation). - Develop the systems, processes, and tools to enable deployment and maintenance of deployed systems. - Source, evaluate, and manage vendors across structured cabling, JIT fiber remediation, rack-and-stack/patching teams, and testing/verification support. - Define scope, develop SOWs, validate pricing, and manage execution to contract terms; track vendor performance against quality, throughput, safety, schedule, and documentation requirements; drive corrective actions when vendors miss standards. - Partner with Network Engineering to resolve field issues and deployment constraints (wrong switch delivered, substitutions, component failures, damaged gear; installation blockers and design/port mapping discrepancies; sequencing changes to maintain schedule). - Partner with Data Center Engineering to align deployments to approved physical network infrastructure designs; verify fiber routing diversity and pathway separation; enforce installation standards for routing, labeling, and cable administration; resolve field constraints that would force deviations from design intent. - Partner with Capacity Delivery / Construction / Facilities to gain site access and coordinate logistics/staging; ensure pathways, trays, penetrations, and build readiness for trunk pulls; align f…

jobs.title: Partner AI Deployment Engineer, Global Advisory Alliances
jobs.body: About the role We are looking for a Partner AI Deployment Engineer (ADE) to own OpenAI’s senior technical engagement with McKinsey, BCG, and Bain globally. This role is the primary technical point of contact for MBB advisory partners on complex enterprise proposals and transformation programmes, ensuring that OpenAI’s platform is positioned, architected, and deployed to support board-level AI agendas. The role sits at the intersection of strategy, architecture, and execution. You will work directly with MBB partners and principals to translate executive ambition into scalable, production-ready AI programmes built on the OpenAI platform. You will operate across multiple industries and engagements, shaping solution direction, guiding architectural decisions, and orchestrating delivery across OpenAI and partner teams. This role is based in Munich or Dublin. We use a hybrid work model of 3 days in the office per week and offer relocation assistance to new employees. In this role, you will: Act as the senior technical lead for McKinsey, BCG, and Bain across priority enterprise pursuits and transformation programmes. - Serve as the primary technical counterpart to MBB Partners and Principals during proposal development and client engagements. - Translate board-level AI strategies into executable technical architectures, delivery plans, and platform roadmaps. - Provide architectural leadership across multiple concurrent programmes, ensuring consistency, quality, and alignment with OpenAI standards. - Orchestrate ADEs and Forward Deployed Engineers (FDEs) across MBB-led engagements, engaging the right expertise at each phase. - Guide partners and customers through critical decisions on platform design, scalability, governance, safety, and reliability. - Support the development of repeatable industry solutions and transformation patterns built on the OpenAI platform. - Act as a technical escalation point during complex or high-risk phases of delivery to reduce execution risk. - Represent MBB partners internally, synthesising insights from large-scale programmes into actionable input for Product and Research teams. You will thrive in this role if you: - Have held a highly technical role at McKinsey, BCG, or Bain (e.g., Partner, Principal, Senior Expert, or equivalent) - preferred. - Have 10+ years of experience in enterprise architecture, AI/ML platforms, solution delivery, or technology strategy. - Bring a strong combination of deep technical expertise and commercial or advisory experience. - Have led or supported large-scale enterprise transformation programmes alongside senior client executives. - Possess industry expertise in at least one of the following: Financial Services; Pharma & Life Sciences; Healthcare; Retail. - Are comfortable operating in ambiguous environments and balancing strategic intent with delivery constraints. - Can engage credibly in boardrooms, executive workshops, and deep technical design sessions. - Take ownership of outcomes an…

jobs.title: Researcher, Frontier Cybersecurity Risks
jobs.body: ABOUT THE TEAM The Safety Systems org ensures that OpenAI’s most capable models can be responsibly developed and deployed. We build evaluations, safeguards, and safety frameworks that help our models behave as intended in real-world settings. The Preparedness team is an important part of the Safety Systems https://openai.com/safety/safety-systems org at OpenAI, and is guided by OpenAI’s Preparedness Framework https://openai.com/index/updating-our-preparedness-framework/. Frontier AI models have the potential to benefit all of humanity, but also pose increasingly severe risks. To ensure that AI promotes positive change, the Preparedness team helps us prepare for the development of increasingly capable frontier AI models. This team is tasked with identifying, tracking, and preparing for catastrophic risks related to frontier AI models. The mission of the Preparedness team is to: 1. Closely monitor and predict the evolving capabilities of frontier AI systems, with an eye towards risks whose impact could be catastrophic 2. Ensure we have concrete procedures, infrastructure and partnerships to mitigate these risks and to safely handle the development of powerful AI systems Preparedness tightly connects capability assessment, evaluations, and internal red teaming, and mitigations for frontier models, as well as overall coordination on AGI preparedness. This is fast paced, exciting work that has far reaching importance for the company and for society. ABOUT THE ROLE Models are becoming increasingly capable—moving from tools that assist humans to agents that can plan, execute, and adapt in the real world. As we push toward AGI, cybersecurity becomes one of the most important and urgent frontiers: the same systems that can accelerate productivity can also accelerate exploitation. As a Researcher for cybersecurity risks, you will help design and implement an end-to-end mitigation stack to reduce severe cyber misuse across OpenAI’s products. This role requires strong technical depth and close cross-functional collaboration to ensure safeguards are enforceable, scalable, and effective. You’ll contribute directly to building protections that remain robust as products, model capabilities, and attacker behaviors evolve. IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL: - Design and implement mitigation components for model-enabled cybersecurity misuse—spanning prevention, monitoring, detection, and enforcement—under the guidance of senior technical and risk leadership. - Integrate safeguards across product surfaces in partnership with product and engineering teams, helping ensure protections are consistent, low-latency, and scale with usage and new model capabilities. - Evaluate technical trade-offs within the cybersecurity risk domain (coverage, latency, model utility, and user privacy) and propose pragmatic, testable solutions. - Collaborate closely with risk and threat modeling partners to align mitigation design with anticipated attacker behaviors and high-impact misuse scenarios. -…

jobs.title: Partner Director, Global BCG Alliance
jobs.body: ABOUT THE TEAM OpenAI’s GTM Partnerships team builds a strategic, global partner ecosystem designed to accelerate customer success, enable enterprise AI adoption, and drive durable growth in support of OpenAI’s mission toward AGI. We work closely across product, research, sales, legal, and communications teams to ensure a cohesive strategy and disciplined execution with our most strategic partners. ABOUT THE ROLE We are hiring a Global Partner Director, BCG Alliance to lead and scale OpenAI’s relationship with Boston Consulting Group. This role serves as the single accountable executive owner of the BCG relationship globally. You will be responsible for defining strategic direction, establishing a durable operating model, and converting alignment into scaled commercial and transformational outcomes across regions and industries. The role carries full responsibility for the health, performance, and long-term expansion of the partnership and requires senior executive presence, judgment, and accountability. IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL: - Own the global BCG relationship, including pipeline growth, enterprise impact, and long-term expansion across priority industries. - Define and run OpenAI’s operating model for the BCG partnership, spanning governance, joint planning, and execution cadence across regions. - Serve as OpenAI’s senior executive counterpart to BCG’s global leadership, including BCG X and relevant practice leaders. - Set joint priorities, resolve escalations, and ensure coordinated execution across global and regional teams. - Shape and lead co-selling of complex, CxO-level enterprise AI transformation opportunities. - Engage directly with enterprise executives (CEO, CDO, CIO, CTO, Board) alongside BCG partners to frame AI strategy, operating models, and value realization. - Coordinate internal OpenAI stakeholders (product, research, GTM, legal, communications) to support BCG-led strategic engagements and lighthouse opportunities. - Establish and enforce clear IP, quality, and conflict-of-interest guardrails across joint engagements and any co-developed assets. - Define vertical strategy and lighthouse account selection in partnership with BCG across priority industries. - Build and lead OpenAI’s dedicated BCG alliance pod over time, spanning partner management, technical leadership, enablement, and operations. - Establish durable reporting, metrics, and operating cadence to ensure consistent alliance performance and continuous improvement. YOU MIGHT THRIVE IN THIS ROLE IF YOU HAVE: - Associate Partner / Principal or Partner-level experience at an MBB firm preferred, or equivalent senior leadership experience in a top-tier strategy and transformation organization. - A strong combination of technology fluency and commercial leadership, ideally across business development, industry advisory, or alliances/partnership roles at top-tier tech company. - Proven ownership of global, multi-year strategic partnerships with executive accountability and…

jobs.title: Partner Director,  Head of Technology Partnerships
jobs.body: ABOUT THE TEAM OpenAI’s GTM Partnerships team builds a strategic, global partner ecosystem to accelerate customer success, enable enterprise AI adoption, and drive durable growth in support of OpenAI’s mission toward AGI. We work cross-functionally across Product, Engineering, Security, Legal, and GTM to deliver strong partner outcomes through disciplined strategy and execution. This role sits in Partnerships/GTM and leads a portfolio of ISV and technology partnerships, with deep collaboration across internal teams to support integrations, joint go-to-market, and long-term partnership growth. ABOUT THE ROLE We are hiring a Partner Director, Head of Technology Partnerships to lead and scale OpenAI’s ISV and technology partner portfolio. You will be responsible for building strategic relationships with leading enterprise platforms and software providers, and translating shared priorities into scalable integrations and go-to-market motions that drive customer adoption. This leader will operate as the single accountable owner for a set of high-impact partnerships, setting direction, establishing operating cadence, and aligning internal and partner stakeholders to deliver measurable business outcomes. Success in this role requires strong judgment, executive presence, and the ability to navigate complex, cross-functional work across product, engineering, security, and GTM. IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL: - Own and grow a portfolio of strategic ISV and technology partnerships, with accountability for partnership health, adoption, and business impact. - Define partnership strategy and priorities, including joint plans, goals, and an execution roadmap across key partner segments. - Establish and run a durable operating model, including governance, stakeholder alignment, escalation paths, and execution cadence. - Partner with Product and Engineering to shape and deliver integrations that meet enterprise requirements for reliability, security, and scalability. - Collaborate with Security, Legal, and Privacy teams to ensure appropriate trust, compliance, and commercial frameworks for joint work. - Build and drive joint GTM plans with Field and Marketing teams, including enablement, solution positioning, and coordinated execution. - Track performance against clear metrics, identify bottlenecks, and drive continuous improvement across partnerships and programs. - Represent OpenAI with senior partner executives, driving alignment, resolving issues, and expanding partnership scope over time. YOU MIGHT THRIVE IN THIS ROLE IF YOU HAVE: - Significant experience leading strategic technology partnerships, alliances, or platform ecosystem programs in enterprise software or cloud. - A track record building and scaling partnerships from early-stage strategy through execution, integrations, and GTM. - Strong technical and product fluency, with the ability to partner effectively with solutions engineering and security teams (without needing to be the hands-on implementer). - Expe…

jobs.title: AI Support Engineer, Government - San Francisco
jobs.body: About the Team OpenAI’s User Operations team shepherds our customers’ adoption of AI and ensures that our customers' product experience is nothing short of exceptional. We are building the very first post-AGI support team. We resolve complex issues, provide technical guidance, and support customers in maximizing value and adoption from deploying our products. We work closely with Sales, Technical Success, Product, Engineering and others, to deliver the best possible experience to our customers at scale. OpenAI's customers represent a range of diverse backgrounds and maturity, from early-stage startups to established global enterprises. About the Role We are looking for dedicated, experienced, and passionate individuals to help solve some of the most difficult problems faced by our customers and build our post-AGI support team with us. In this role, you will interact directly with customers through support tickets and Slack messages, troubleshooting complex issues and resolving novel, often undefined technical problems while setting a positive precedent for the rest of the team. You’ll partner closely with cross-functional teams to drive initiatives that reduce bugs, improve features, and build the systems that elevate our customer experience. Your work will bring us toward industry-leading response times and service levels, while strengthening our internal customer feedback operations in an increasingly intricate space. You will help scale our support organization by improving operational processes and leveraging our own technology to build the next version of the support team in the new AI world. You will be crucial to the success of the most innovative, disruptive, and high-scale AI solutions being built with OpenAI. If you thrive in environments that value impact, collaboration, and fast-paced problem-solving, you might be the perfect fit for our team We use a hybrid work model of 3 days in the office per week and offer relocation assistance to new employees. As employees in this role with work with many customers based on the East Coast, regularly weekday hours will commence at 6am Pacific. To comply with a U.S. government contract, applicants for this position must be U.S. citizens, U.S. nationals, or U.S. persons (i.e., U.S. lawful permanent residents, refugees and asylees). You’ll be responsible for: - Support public sector customers, including U.S. Government agencies and teams operating under FedRAMP requirements, by troubleshooting complex issues and translating security and compliance needs into clear next steps. - Work directly with customers, solving their most complex problems and providing ownership and education on the use of our platforms. - Be among the foremost experts on everything related to OpenAI products, even where our AI does not have the answer. - Be among the last line of defense before our core Product and Engineering teams, and partner with engineering and customer teams to resolve issues. - Use scripting and emergin…

jobs.title: Sourcing Delivery Lead
jobs.body: About the Team Our Procurement team enables OpenAI to scale responsibly, efficiently, and at speed. We partner closely with stakeholders to accelerate business priorities while maintaining strong commercial rigor, compliance, and operational excellence. We run the high volume sourcing engine across the company, including quick turn negotiations, rapid cycle times, complete diligence packages, consistent contract quality, and strong vendor controls. The Sourcing Delivery team ensures Strategic Sourcing can scale without slowing down the business. About the Role As the Sourcing Delivery Lead, you will run the high volume negotiation and contracting engine that keeps the business moving. You will support routine, lower spend, lower complexity commercial work that is often time-sensitive and operationally critical. Your team’s speed, judgment, and customer service will directly unblock teams and keep OpenAI operating at pace. You will lead the front-end of sourcing engagement as a trusted sourcing partner, guiding requests from intake through negotiation and signature. This is not a transactional, system-processing role. It is a commercial leadership function that pairs operational discipline with strong judgment to deliver fast cycle times, predictable SLAs, and consistently high contract quality at scale. You will lead a team of Category Coordinators, with a Contract Delivery Lead reporting to you, and you will run the sourcing execution layer from first stakeholder engagement through signature, moving quickly within clear guardrails and escalating cleanly when required. You will serve Strategic Sourcing Leads as a core execution partner, turning strategy into practical playbooks and executing high volume, low risk negotiations so they can stay focused on complex deals and long-term priorities. You will partner closely with Procurement Operations, who owns the enabling systems, policy, and process infrastructure, while your team owns stakeholder engagement, negotiation execution, and contracting throughput. You will also partner closely with Legal to build and maintain contracting playbooks, a clause library, and approved fallback positions that keep standard agreements moving quickly within guardrails. We’re looking for a leader who has built and run high volume procurement execution and contracting teams in fast-scaling environments, and who brings a high bar for speed, quality, and stakeholder experience. This role is based in San Francisco, CA. We use a hybrid work model of 3 days in the office per week and offer relocation assistance to new employees. Travel is expected up to twice per year to support distributed delivery operations. You’ll be responsible for: - Lead and scale a high-performing sourcing execution organization (Category Coordinators, onsite and nearshore commercial negotiators, plus a Contract Delivery Lead), setting clear standards for speed, quality, and stakeholder experience across high-volume, lower-complexity work. - Desi…

jobs.title: Consumer Marketing Manager, India
jobs.body: About the Role: We are seeking an experienced and versatile consumer marketing manager for India to drive our marketing strategy and execution. This is a key role in our India marketing team for a full-stack marketer with a deep understanding of the Indian landscape—someone who can build and scale locally resonant campaigns and programs across multiple audiences with a heavy focus on consumers. This person will be responsible for shaping and executing integrated marketing initiatives that fuel adoption, build trust, and grow the brand in India. They will bring strong expertise in consumer marketing while also being comfortable navigating complex stakeholder environments and leading cross-functional programs. This role requires someone with deep market knowledge, a strong local network of media, agency and production partners, and a bias for action in fast-moving contexts. The role can be based in Delhi, Mumbai or Bangalore. Key Responsibilities: - Lead the development and execution of India-specific marketing strategies, ensuring they are locally grounded and aligned with global objectives. - Drive full-funnel marketing initiatives across online and offline channels with scaled marketing campaigns - Act as a local expert and thought partner—bringing deep insights about Indian culture, user behavior, and media landscapes to shape strategy and creative. - Manage and collaborate with local agencies and production partners to deliver culturally relevant, high-quality campaigns and content. - Partner cross-functionally with product marketing, comms, partnerships, and regional leadership to deliver integrated programs that drive measurable outcomes. - Represent the voice of India internally, ensuring local nuance and context are reflected in marketing strategy, messaging, and execution. Qualifications: - 10-12 years of experience in marketing, with a strong track record of delivering high-impact campaigns in India across multiple audience segments including consumer - Deep understanding of the Indian consumer landscape, cultural context, and regional media and marketing dynamics. - Proven ability to build and execute integrated marketing plans across brand, digital, influencer, and partner channels. - Comfortable operating at both strategic and executional levels in a fast-paced, rapidly evolving environment. - Strong network of local agencies, creatives, and production vendors; proven experience managing local partners. - Excellent communicator and collaborator, with the ability to influence cross-functional teams and external stakeholders. About OpenAI OpenAI is an AI research and deployment company dedicated to ensuring that general-purpose artificial intelligence benefits all of humanity. We push the boundaries of the capabilities of AI systems and seek to safely deploy them to the world through our products. AI is an extremely powerful tool that must be created with safety and human needs at its core, and to achieve our mission, we must encompass…

jobs.title: Data Scientist, B2B
jobs.body: About the Team Our B2B product organization is building the systems that will accelerate and automate how companies operate using AI coworkers.Our vision is to enable companies to scale to $1B+ with teams of fewer than five people by leveraging AI agents and automation. We are building the infrastructure, products, and analytics that allow organizations to deploy fleets of AI agents, automate workflows, and dramatically increase employee productivity. This team sits at the center of the future of work: defining how humans collaborate with AI systems across companies, workflows, and knowledge. About the Role We are looking for a product-focused Data Scientist to help shape the next generation of AI-powered enterprise products, including ChatGPT Enterprise and agentic automation systems. This is not a traditional analytics role. You will work directly with product managers, engineers, and researchers to define how AI agents behave, how organizations adopt them, and how we measure their impact on work. You’ll analyze how users interact with AI systems, inform product strategy, and help build the measurement frameworks that guide the future of enterprise AI. In this role, you will: - Partner closely with product, engineering, and research teams to shape AI-powered enterprise products - Define metrics and measurement frameworks for AI agents, automation workflows, and enterprise adoption - Analyze how organizations use AI tools and identify opportunities to improve product experience and business impact - Drive insights around enterprise adoption, retention, and value realization - Inform product decisions around agent orchestration, enterprise workflows, and AI productivity tools - Design experiments and evaluations that improve AI system performance and user outcomes - Help define how companies operate with AI coworkers and automated workflows What We’re Looking For Must Have: - Strong product analytics or data science experience - Deep curiosity about AI systems and how they change how work gets done - Ability to work closely with product and engineering teams in 0→1 product environments - Strong analytical skills (experimentation, behavioral analysis, product metrics) - Builder mindset — someone who wants to shape products, not just analyze them Nice to Have: - Experience with B2B SaaS or enterprise analytics - Experience with developer platforms, APIs, or technical products - Background in AI/ML systems, agent frameworks, or LLM-based products - Systems thinking around how complex tools and workflows operate at scale Who Thrives Here: - AI-native — actively experimenting with and building with AI tools - Product thinkers who want to shape the future of work - Builders who enjoy early-stage ambiguity and 0→1 problems - Operators who can work independently and drive large parts of a product area Impact You will help define: - How organizations deploy and manage AI agents - How AI transforms enterprise productivity - The measurement systems behind A…

jobs.title: Lead to Opportunity, Data Systems Engineer
jobs.body: About the team OpenAI’s mission is to build safe artificial general intelligence (AGI) which benefits all of humanity. This long-term undertaking brings the world’s best scientists, engineers, and business professionals into one lab together to accomplish this. In pursuit of this mission, our Enterprise Platform team is responsible for enabling our Sales teams as they help customers deploy our highly capable AI products across their business. The team is made of Sales, Solutions, Customer Success, Support, Marketing, and Partnership professionals that work together to create valuable solutions that will help bring AI to as many users as possible. About the role Our GTM team exists to help customers understand the transformational potential of OpenAI’s models, and we believe our own systems should reflect that same intelligence. We’re looking for a Salesforce Engineer to partner with Growth, Marketing, and Sales to build systems that power customer engagement, lifecycle activation, and pipeline creation—supported by strong data enrichment and orchestration across the GTM stack. The primary charter of this role is lead management, sales and marketing engagement workflows, and the enrichment and orchestration of customer data across multiple sources—ensuring Salesforce and adjacent GTM systems are powered by accurate, actionable data to support pipeline creation at scale. In this role, you'll: - Build Lead-to-Opportunity solutions in Salesforce: Develop user experiences, automations, and data enrichment workflows that help capture, qualify, and route inbound and outbound demand efficiently—ensuring leads are enriched with accurate firmographic, technographic, and behavioral data and progressed through early pipeline stages with strong governance. - Engineer integrations across the top-of-funnel stack: Connect Salesforce with marketing automation platforms, enrichment providers, sales engagement tools, routing engines, and event-driven data sources—supporting the orchestration of customer and account data across multiple upstream and downstream systems to enable accurate lead management and timely Marketing-to-Sales handoff. - Test, troubleshoot, and document: Resolve production issues impacting lead capture, routing, enrichment, and sales engagement workflows while maintaining clear documentation aligned with scalable intake, change management, and deployment best practices for data movement across GTM systems. - Partner cross-functionally: Collaborate daily with GTM, Marketing, Growth, Product, and Data teams to design scalable Salesforce-based solutions that support prospecting, pipeline generation, and early-stage customer lifecycle engagement—powered by orchestrated and enriched data across the GTM stack You might thrive in this role if you have: - Advanced Salesforce development (Apex, async patterns, APIs) and data model expertise across opportunity, quote, order, and billing domains. - Proven experience designing and implementing robust integ…

jobs.title: Platform Engineer, Forward Deployed Engineering (FDE) - NYC
jobs.body: About the team OpenAI’s Forward Deployed Engineering (FDE) org sits at the intersection of product, engineering, research, and go-to-market. We take frontier platform capabilities into the real world with design partners, turning raw customer signal into shipped software, repeatable patterns, and durable products. The FDE Platform team is primarily a leverage function that scales the FDE org’s impact to OpenAI’s platform and products. We provide hands-on leverage by embedding with customer-tagged FDE pods to aid in architecting, product shaping, refactoring, and building. This team is perfect for highly collaborative software engineers who love innovating on cutting-edge products with other builders. About the role Platform Engineer is a role within Forward Deployed Engineering (FDE) for strong software and ML engineers who want to build new platform capabilities from scratch, grounded in real customer deployments. You will partner with customer-tagged FDEs who are driving delivery and customer outcomes, and embed where you can provide the highest leverage. In practice that means working in the trenches on architecture, product shaping, refactoring, hardening, and reusable abstractions, while preserving the pod’s ownership of customer understanding and day-to-day execution. You will also collaborate closely with our B2B Platform Team and other long-term owners to align early on what should generalize, what should remain customer-specific, and what “ready for handoff” looks like. This role does not require travel. It is based in San Francisco or New York. We use a hybrid work model of 3 days in the office per week. We offer relocation assistance. Travel is optional-by-project and typically <10%, with occasional spikes for key embeds or launches. In this role you will - Provide hands-on leverage to customer pods: embed with customer-tagged FDE teams to support generalization, contributing directly in architecture, product shaping, refactoring, and implementation. - Turn repeated signals into platform bets: translate cross-customer patterns into crisp hypotheses with clear success criteria, scope, and a validation plan that fits real account constraints. - Raise the engineering bar through tooling and mentorship: set org-wide quality norms through high-signal code review and pairing, and build lightweight developer tooling that makes good architecture, readability, and correctness the default across FDE. - Collaborate as part of cross-functional platform teams: partner closely with B2B Product, customer-tagged FDEs, ops, and business partners to bring the right products and platform capabilities to market. - Lead complex platform capabilities end-to-end when needed: for high-leverage primitives like our Context Platform, act as DRI from requirements through implementation, make key tradeoffs explicit, and pull in customer pods early to keep the work grounded in real deployments. You might thrive in this role if you - Bring 5+ years of software engin…

jobs.title: Director of Finance, Strategic Partnerships
jobs.body: ABOUT THE TEAM OpenAI’s Strategic Finance organization provides financial insights and guidance to support the company’s long-term strategy and ambitious growth. We partner across Product, Partnerships, Engineering, and GTM to allocate resources to the highest-impact opportunities while protecting sustainable unit economics. The Applications Technology Strategic Finance team partners closely with Product to scale core offerings and bring new products to market. We lead revenue forecasting and performance management across the product portfolio, including economic margins, pricing, and partnership deal pricing and structuring. ABOUT THE ROLE We are hiring a Strategic Finance Partnerships lead to drive strategic decision-making across OpenAI’s growth and media partnership portfolio. This role will be a critical finance partner to Partnerships deal leads and Product owners, responsible for evaluating, pricing, and structuring partnerships, and translating partner opportunity into decision-ready economics and performance management. The role reports to the Head of App Tech Finance and will be hands-on in deal evaluation, negotiation support, and portfolio oversight. You will shape how OpenAI approaches partnership unit economics, measurement and incrementality, and how partnership performance is incorporated into forecasts and planning. This role is based in San Francisco, CA, with a hybrid work model (3 days/week in-office). Relocation assistance is available. IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL: - Own financial evaluation for growth and media partnerships, including modeling revenue, margin, compute, and payback to support decision-making and negotiations. - Develop clear economic frameworks and deal guardrails (pricing floors, rev-share structures, caps, minimum guarantees, performance-based terms) to protect long-term unit economics. - Partner closely with Product and Partnerships to define and measure incrementality and ensure contract terms align with measurable outcomes. - Lead partnership deal pricing and structuring from early-stage strategy through final term-sheet negotiations, including executive-ready recommendations and tradeoffs. - Drive partnership portfolio performance management: establish core metrics, identify leading indicators, track variance to expectations, and surface risks and opportunities. - Integrate partnership economics into revenue forecasting and planning cycles in partnership with the broader team. - Identify recurring contract and measurement risks and establish internal governance and review mechanisms. YOU MIGHT THRIVE IN THIS ROLE IF YOU HAVE: - 12+ years of experience in investment banking, consulting, or buy-side investing coupled with operational experience at a fast-paced, scaling company. - A strong ability to critically evaluate opportunities and risks in financials. - Expert modeling skills, including building detailed product P&Ls and operating models. - The ability to distill complex financial data into actionable in…

jobs.title: Product Marketing Manager, SMB
jobs.body: About the Team The B2B Marketing team contributes to OpenAI’s broader mission of ensuring responsible and widespread adoption of artificial intelligence. We are responsible for developing and executing strategies that drive awareness, engagement, and usage for OpenAI’s products and platform. We take a data-driven approach to understand our customers' needs and challenges, ensuring that their voices are reflected in product development and messaging. We then partner closely with Product, Engineering, Growth, Sales, Research, Comms, and Design teams to create a cohesive customer experience across all our channels. About the Role As a Product Marketing Manager (PMM) for small businesses (SMB), you will define and drive marketing strategies that fuel adoption, engagement, and long-term growth across OpenAI’s product portfolio serving small business customers. This includes ChatGPT Business, our API/Platform, Codex, and new products and features designed to help small businesses build, automate, and scale with AI. This role calls for a strategic marketer who can craft compelling narratives by deeply understanding both OpenAI’s products and the evolving needs of small businesses — from founders and business owners to technical builders. You will translate complex technical functionality into clear, role-based value across a range of SMB use cases. You will drive go-to-market strategy for the SMB segment across multiple products, helping ensure small businesses thrive wherever they are in their AI adoption journey: from first experimentation to scaled deployment. This includes shaping positioning, segmentation, messaging, and lifecycle strategy that drives acquisition, activation, expansion, and retention within the SMB customer base. This role is based in San Francisco, CA. We use a hybrid work model of 3 days in the office per week and offer relocation assistance to new employees. In this role, you will: - Develop and execute SMB-focused go-to-market strategies across ChatGPT Business, the OpenAI API, Codex, and new products and feature launches. - Define segmentation, positioning, and messaging tailored to small business personas across industries, roles, and levels of technical maturity. - Collaborate with product and research teams to translate complex technical concepts into clear, user-centric messaging. - Leverage market research and competitive analysis to inform product positioning and identify growth opportunities. - Create and manage marketing campaigns, content, and materials that grow engagement, adoption and revenue. - Analyze product usage data and user feedback to refine marketing strategies and improve user experience. You might thrive in this role if you: - Have 12+ years of experience in product marketing or segment marketing within technology or SaaS. - Have experience marketing platform, developer, or product-led offerings to SMB or mid-market audiences. - Have a strong understanding of AI technologies and the ability to articulate…

jobs.title: Recruiter, AI/ML Research
jobs.body: About the Team OpenAI’s mission is to build safe artificial general intelligence (AGI) that benefits all of humanity. Achieving this requires bringing the world’s most exceptional talent under one roof to push the boundaries of what’s possible. Our Research Recruiting team plays a critical role in this effort. We are an embedded part of the research organization, working side by side with our research staff to deeply understand evolving priorities, build trust, and strategically shape the future of OpenAI’s talent. About the Role You will own and execute long-term talent strategies to identify, engage, and recruit many of the world’s leading and emerging AI researchers, research engineers, and technical scientists working at the frontier of machine learning. This is not a traditional execution-focused recruiting role. You will operate as a strategic partner to OpenAI’s research staff, helping define hiring priorities, shape search strategy, influence candidate evaluation, and guide hiring decisions that directly impact the direction and quality of our frontier-model research and fulfillment of our mission. In this role, you will: - Partner directly with research and technical staff to define hiring priorities, shape search strategies, and anticipate future talent needs as technical roadmaps evolve. - Proactively identify and cultivate exceptional AI/ML research talent across industry, academia, and emerging labs, often before formal hiring needs exist. - Use market insights and candidate signals to influence hiring decisions, leveling, and compensation strategy for highly specialized research roles. - Serve as a trusted advisor throughout candidate evaluation and closing — helping leaders calibrate for research excellence, long-term potential, and organizational fit. - Collaborate closely with your sourcing partner to execute complex, high-impact searches in ambiguous or rapidly evolving technical domains. You might thrive in this role if you: - Significant experience recruiting within highly technical or specialized environments. - Deep interest in AI research and a desire to engage directly with global research communities. - Experience recruiting within highly technical or specialized environments such as ML/AI, distributed systems, infrastructure, scientific computing, or quantitative research. - Track record of leading complex, ambiguous technical searches from early talent mapping through close. - Experience navigating high-stakes negotiations with senior technical or research candidates. - Comfort operating in fast-moving environments where hiring priorities and role definitions may evolve over time. Workplace & Location This role is based in our San Francisco office and we aren’t considering remote applications at this time. We use a hybrid work model of 3 days in the office with optional work from home on Thursdays and Fridays. We also offer relocation assistance to new employees. Our open-plan offices have height-adjustable desks, confe…

jobs.title: Platform Engineering Manager, Forward Deployed Engineering (FDE)
jobs.body: About the team OpenAI’s Forward Deployed Engineering (FDE) org sits at the intersection of product, engineering, research, and go-to-market. We take frontier platform capabilities into the real world with design partners, turning raw customer signal into shipped software, repeatable patterns, and durable products. The FDE Platform team is primarily a leverage function that scales the FDE org’s impact to OpenAI’s platform and products. We provide hands-on leverage by embedding with customer-tagged FDE pods to aid in architecting, product shaping, refactoring, and building. This team is perfect for highly collaborative software engineers who love innovating on cutting-edge products with other builders. Your mandate is to increase the rate at which customer-grounded ideas become durable, generalizable platform bets, by providing hands-on product and engineering leverage to customer-tagged pods. You will partner with customer-tagged FDEs who are driving delivery and customer outcomes, and embed Platform FDE capacity where they can provide the highest leverage.
